subject: Cloud Computing
title: What Is Edge Computing?
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on What Is Edge Computing?.

hook.text: The cloud is moving closer to you. Edge computing puts processing power near the devices that need it most.

buildup.text: Sending data to a distant data center adds latency. For self-driving cars or AR glasses, milliseconds matter.

discovery.text: Edge nodes process data locally — at cell towers, factories, or retail stores — and sync results with the cloud later.

twist.text: Edge computing doesn't replace the cloud. It extends it. The cloud becomes a hierarchy: edge, region, and central.

climax.text: By 2025, Gartner predicts 75% of enterprise data will be processed outside traditional data centers. The edge is here.

punchline.text: The cloud is moving to your doorstep. Closer means faster.

quiz.question: Why is edge computing important for applications like self-driving cars?
options: It processes data locally, reducing the latency that distant data centers introduce
Self-driving cars have no internet connection at all
Edge computing is cheaper than cloud computing for all use cases
